rag_rules: Fix type mismatches in AGREE expressions by ensuring arithmetic operands and comparisons use consistent types (int, real, bool).
Resolve all AGREE references to named entities (e.g., Brake_Mode, Brake_Cmd) by ensuring they are declared or visible in the same annex scope.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always ensure type consistency between operands in binary expressions and comparisons.
When referencing identifiers in AGREE, verify that they are declared or visible within the same annex block.
Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations in AGREE contracts to avoid '<error>' type issues.
Prefer explicit type declarations (int, real, bool) in AGREE const and eq statements to prevent resolution errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and placed in valid component type or implementation scopes.
Component type contracts should avoid implementation-specific subcomponent paths; use only visible features or variables.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in AGREE unless explicitly defined in the model's dependencies.
Avoid placing AGREE assumptions or guarantees in implementation scopes unless they refer to implementation-level bindings.
